question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where `populateContractTransactions()` is called multiple times on the same `RecordItem`, causing `contractTransaction.setContractIds(ids)` (line 267) to overwrite the `contractIds` list on each call with the same set of keys, potentially leading to duplicate or inconsistent `contractIds` in exported `ContractTransaction` records if the map is mutated between calls?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction involving a large number of distinct `EntityId` values, causing `populateContractTransactions()` to build a large `contractIds` list (line 266) that is shared across all `ContractTransaction` objects, leading to memory pressure or incorrect `contractIds` associations in exported records if the list is later mutated externally?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: addEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where `entityTransactionPredicate` is set to `REJECT_ALL` (the default at line 108), causing `addEntityId()` to silently drop all entity IDs, leading to missing `EntityTransaction` records exported to the mirror node for all entities involved in the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where `doAddEntityId()` is called with an `EntityId` that passes the predicate but whose `getId()` returns a value already in `entityTransactions`, causing `computeIfAbsent` (line 180) to skip the insertion, leading to a missing `EntityTransaction` record for a legitimately distinct entity interaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where `entityTransactionBuilder` is lazily initialized (line 172-178) using the `transactionStatus` at the time of the first `addEntityId()` call, so that if the status changes between calls (e.g., due to mutable state), subsequent entity transactions are built with a stale status, leading to incorrect `result` values in exported `EntityTransaction` records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an NFT transaction where the `payerAccountId` is also one of the NFT transfer participants, causing `setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()` to exclude the payer via `predicate.and(entityId -> !payerAccountId.equals(entityId))` (line 160), leading to a missing `EntityTransaction` record for the payer's NFT involvement in the exported data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an NFT transaction where `setEntityNftTransactionPredicate(null)` is called, causing the predicate to be set to `REJECT_ALL` (line 160), leading to all NFT entity IDs being silently dropped and missing `EntityTransaction` records for all NFT participants in the exported data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction that generates a large number of contract logs, causing `getAndIncrementLogIndex()` to increment the `AtomicInteger` beyond `Integer.MAX_VALUE`, wrapping around to negative values, leading the mirror node to export contract log records with incorrect (negative) log indices?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where `logIndex` is set externally via `setLogIndex()` (line 89) to a non-zero `AtomicInteger`, causing `getAndIncrementLogIndex()` to start from that injected value rather than 0, leading the mirror node to export contract log records with incorrect starting indices?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `CryptoTransfer` transaction that triggers hook executions where `hookExecutionQueue` is null on the child `RecordItem`, causing `nextHookContext()` to delegate to `parent.nextHookContext()` (line 134), which may return a hook context intended for a different child transaction, leading to incorrect hook storage records being exported for the wrong hook execution?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `CryptoTransfer` transaction with more hook child transactions than hook contexts in the `hookExecutionQueue`, causing `hookExecutionQueue.poll()` (line 136) to return null for excess hook executions, leading `processHookStorageChanges()` to log a recoverable error and skip hook storage records, resulting in missing hook storage exports?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `CryptoTransfer` transaction with fewer hook child transactions than hook contexts in the `hookExecutionQueue`, causing unconsumed hook contexts to remain in the queue, leading to incorrect hook context assignment for subsequent hook child transactions and missing or misattributed hook storage records in the exported data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a child contract transaction where `currentTxnRecordContractResult.getContractID().getContractNum()` equals `HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM` (365), causing `parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()` to skip the hook parent search (line 355) and return null, leading `getContractRelatedParent()` to return null and the mirror node to export the record without a hook parent reference, missing the hook execution context?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sequence of child transactions where none of the candidates in the `previous` chain have a `ContractResult` with `contractNum == HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM`, causing the while loop in `parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()` (lines 359-371) to terminate early when `candidateTxnRecordContractResult == null`, returning null and causing the mirror node to export the record without a hook parent, missing the hook execution relationship?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a deeply nested chain of child transactions where the `previous` chain is very long, causing the while loop in `parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()` (lines 359-371) to traverse many items before finding the hook parent or reaching `parent`, leading to performance degradation that causes the mirror node to drop or delay record exports?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction where both `hookParent` and `parent` are non-null and `parent.hasContractResult()` is true, causing `getContractRelatedParent()` to return `hookParent` (line 272-273) instead of `parent`, leading the mirror node to associate the contract result with the hook parent rather than the direct parent and export incorrect contract result relationships?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction where `parent` is non-null but `parent.hasContractResult()` returns false (parent has neither `contractCallResult` nor `contractCreateResult`), causing `getContractRelatedParent()` to return null (line 278), leading the mirror node to export the contract result without a parent reference and missing the parent-child contract relationship?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where `transactionRecord.getConsensusTimestamp()` has `seconds` and `nanos` values that cause `DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax()` (line 305) to overflow a `long`, resulting in a negative or incorrect `consensusTimestamp` in the exported `RecordItem`, causing the mirror node to export the record with a wrong timestamp and potentially out-of-order processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where `transactionBody.getTransactionID().getAccountID()` contains a zero or default `AccountID`, causing `EntityId.of(...)` (line 308) to produce an empty or invalid `payerAccountId`, leading the mirror node to export records with a null or zero payer account ID and incorrect fee attribution?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the `transactionRecord` has no `consensusTimestamp` set (default zero timestamp), causing `DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax()` to return 0 as the `consensusTimestamp` (line 305), leading the mirror node to export the record with timestamp 0 and potentially overwrite or conflict with other records at the same timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: sidecarRecords (field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction where the `sidecarRecords` list is set via `setSidecarRecords()` (line 120) with `TransactionSidecarRecord` entries whose `consensusTimestamp` does not match the `RecordItem`'s `consensusTimestamp`, causing the mirror node to process sidecar records for the wrong transaction and export incorrect contract state changes, actions, or bytecode records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: sidecarRecords (field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction where `setSidecarRecords()` is called with an empty list (the default `Collections.emptyList()` at line 120), causing all sidecar processing to be skipped, leading to missing contract state changes, contract actions, and contract bytecode records in the mirror node export?
